_note: ip-hygiene:ignore-file — This file contains paraphrased standards language (HIPAA, SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria) used as few-shot exemplars for AI output. All quoted standards text is paraphrased/summarized; any remaining near-verbatim phrasing is permitted under fair-use for internal model prompting. Entries without an 'output' field are metadata and ignored by the loader.

description: Audit finding draft for a SOC 2 Type II engagement — missing vulnerability management process
input_summary: Auditor found no formal vulnerability scanning program. Target environment: cloud SaaS, AWS-hosted. Prior-year finding does not exist.

output.title: Absence of Formal Vulnerability Management Program
output.criteria: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria CC7.1 requires that the entity use detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that introduce new vulnerabilities. CC7.2 requires management of vulnerabilities and application security threats on an ongoing basis. An implemented vulnerability management program with periodic scanning, remediation SLAs, and evidence of remediation is required.
output.condition: During the audit period (January 1 – December 31, 2024), ControlWeave observed no evidence of a documented vulnerability management policy, vulnerability scanning configuration, or remediation tracking register. Inquiry with the DevOps team confirmed ad-hoc patching triggered only by public advisories rather than a systematic scanning cadence.
output.cause: No formal ownership of the vulnerability management function has been assigned. Security tooling (e.g., Amazon Inspector, Trivy, Snyk) is available but not configured to scan production workloads on a recurring schedule.
output.effect: Without systematic vulnerability identification and tracking, critical vulnerabilities may remain undetected and unmitigated for extended periods, increasing the risk of unauthorized access, data breach, and material disruption to services. Exploitable vulnerabilities identified in penetration testing may not be remediated within acceptable timeframes.
output.recommendation:
1. Appoint a vulnerability management owner (ISSM or equivalent).
2. Deploy automated scanning of all production infrastructure on at least a weekly cadence using a tool such as Amazon Inspector, Tenable, or Qualys.
3. Define and document remediation SLAs: Critical ≤15 days, High ≤30 days, Medium ≤90 days.
4. Establish a vulnerability tracking register reviewed monthly by the security team.
5. Produce a written Vulnerability Management Policy approved by senior management.
output.management_response_placeholder: Management agrees with the finding. Target remediation: [DATE]. Owner: [NAME/ROLE].

description: Audit finding for a NIST 800-53 engagement — access review not performed within required cadence
input_summary: Agency environment. AC-2(2) requires automated account removal for temporary/emergency accounts. Auditor found 34 accounts not reviewed in 18 months.

output.title: User Access Reviews Not Conducted Within Required Frequency — 34 Stale Accounts Identified
output.criteria: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 AC-2(2) requires the organization to automatically remove temporary and emergency accounts after an organization-defined time period. AC-2(3) requires automatic disabling of inactive accounts after [90 days]. The current System Security Plan specifies quarterly access reviews.
output.condition: Of the 412 active accounts in the system, 34 accounts (8.3%) have had no access review activity in the past 18 months. Additionally, 6 temporary contractor accounts created for a Q1 2023 project were not disabled upon project completion and remain active as of the audit inquiry date (November 2024).
output.cause: The quarterly access review process is manual and depends on system owner availability. During periods of high operational tempo (Q3 2023, Q2 2024), reviews were deprioritized. No automated workflow or reminder system exists to enforce the cadence.
output.effect: Stale accounts represent a potential attack surface for credential theft and misuse. The 6 active contractor accounts have elevated read access to controlled unclassified information (CUI) without a current business justification, which may violate FIPS 199 confidentiality requirements and applicable data sharing agreements.
output.recommendation:
1. Immediately disable the 6 identified contractor accounts pending business justification review.
2. Implement automated access review workflows using the organization's existing ITSM tooling to trigger quarterly access certification campaigns.
3. Configure the IAM platform to automatically flag accounts with zero activity for 90 days for administrator review and disable after 120 days.
4. Update the SSP to reflect the automated controls and document exceptions.
5. Conduct a one-time sweep of all remaining 34 stale accounts within 30 days.
output.management_response_placeholder: Management concurs. POA&M entry created. Target: [DATE]. Owner: [NAME/ROLE].
evidence_of_exception: 6 contractor accounts active without current SOW
